title: Pogonospermum incanum
intro: Pogonospermum incanum, also known as Incan Plum or Pogonanthus, is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pogonospermum incanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Pogonospermum incanum is classified within the family Rutaceae. This plant belongs to the order Sapindales and is a member of the genus Pogonospermum. Pogonospermum incanum is native to tropical Asia. The species is characterized by its small, yellow flowers and its aromatic leaves. It is a relatively small shrub that is often used in traditional medicine.
